summary: Migrated /pages/projects/outcomes/ to a GOV.UK Frontend Nunjucks source template, registered it with the GOV.UK page renderer, replaced browser alert validation with a GOV.UK error summary path, and rendered the impact table through the GOV.UK table macro.

task.request: Make /pages/projects/outcomes/ use a Nunjucks template with macros and render a fully compliant GOV.UK Frontend UI.

decisions.decision: Create src/govuk/templates/pages/projects-outcomes.njk as the source template for /pages/projects/outcomes/.
decisions.reason: Recent GOV.UK migrations use Nunjucks as source of truth, with generated public HTML produced by the renderer workflow.

decisions.decision: Use GOV.UK macros for breadcrumbs, buttons, error summary, input, inset text, select, table and textarea.
decisions.reason: The page is a form-led GOV.UK Frontend page and should use component macros rather than hand-coded mixed CSS fragments.

decisions.decision: Do not manually edit public/pages/projects/outcomes/index.html in this branch.
decisions.reason: The repository render workflow commits generated GOV.UK page outputs after template changes, and index.html files are generated artefacts.

decisions.decision: Keep the outcomes breadcrumb current-page label as Impact & ROI.
decisions.reason: Existing route-state tests and user expectations rely on that page label.

decisions.decision: Remove the action-bar Back to Project button from the outcomes template.
decisions.reason: The user explicitly rejected that UI. The page now relies on breadcrumbs for project navigation.

decisions.decision: Replace alert-based validation with a GOV.UK error summary path in impact-tracker.js.
decisions.reason: A fully compliant GOV.UK form should surface validation through page content, links to fields and accessible error-summary behaviour rather than browser alerts.

changes: Added projects-outcomes.njk with GOV.UK Frontend component macros and retained existing runtime IDs.
Updated impact-tracker.js to populate #impact-error-summary for missing metric name validation instead of calling alert().
Rendered the impact table through govukTable rather than hand-coded table markup.
Updated govuk-forms route-state expectations so outcomes is no longer treated as a legacy govuk-forms.css route.
Updated breadcrumb/back-link route-state expectations so outcomes keeps the breadcrumb marker but does not require back-to-project.

validation.localValidation: Not run in this connector-only session.
validation.automationExpected: The Render GOV.UK pages workflow should render public/pages/projects/outcomes/index.html after source updates.

residualRisks: No browser preview was available in this connector-only session, so focus management and form submission still need preview verification.
nextValidationTargets: Confirm the rendered outcomes page no longer imports legacy GOV.UK partial CSS or /css/screen.css.
Confirm the rendered outcomes page contains Impact & ROI and no back-to-project button.
Submit the impact form with Metric name empty and confirm #impact-error-summary links to #impact-metricName.
Submit a valid impact record and confirm the impact table refreshes.
